task: I need to send a quick update to a customer about a delay, and I want to make sure it covers the key facts without leaving anything important out. Can you draft a short message I could send?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a short, factual template you can send:

**Hi [Customer Name],**  
I wanted to share a quick update on your delivery. We know it’s delayed, and the current cause is the storm impacting operations. At this time, we don’t have a confirmed new delivery window yet. The next update will come from [team/person] by [time].  
Thanks for your patience.

If you want, I can also make this **more direct**, **more apologetic**, or **shorter**.

user_simulator.text:
Pretty close, but I’d want it to say a little more than just “storm impacting operations.” Customers usually want the part that matters to them — what’s delayed, whether anything is still moving, and when they can expect the next update. Also, I’d rather not sound too vague.

Try this style:

Hi [Customer Name],

Quick update on your order: the storm has delayed part of our shipping schedule, so your delivery will not arrive as originally planned. We’re checking route and warehouse status now, and we’ll send you a specific update by [time/date].

I appreciate your patience, and we’ll keep you posted as soon as we have a firmer schedule.

If you can make it a little tighter and more customer-friendly without sounding fluffy, that’d be better.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter, more customer-friendly version:

**Hi [Customer Name],**

Quick update on your order: the storm has delayed part of our shipping schedule, so your delivery won’t arrive as originally planned. We’re checking the route and warehouse status now, and we’ll send you a specific update by [time/date].

Thanks for your patience — we’ll keep you posted as soon as we have a firmer schedule.

If you want, I can also make it:
- a little **more direct**
- a little **warmer**
- or even **shorter for text/SMS**

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Tight enough, still clear, not mushy.
